# Implementation Plan: MVP RSS Reader — Subscription Management

**Branch**: `001-mvp-rss-reader` | **Date**: 2026-04-14 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/001-mvp-rss-reader/spec.md`

**Note**: This file was generated by the `/speckit.plan` command.

## Summary

Build a minimal proof-of-concept RSS/Atom feed reader that allows a single local user to add feed subscriptions by URL and view the accumulated subscription list. The backend is an ASP.NET Core Web API that manages an in-memory subscription list; the frontend is a Blazor WebAssembly app that renders the subscription input and list. No feed fetching, URL validation, or persistence is in scope for this MVP.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: C# / .NET 10 LTS  
**Primary Dependencies**: ASP.NET Core Web API (backend), Blazor WebAssembly (frontend)  
**Storage**: In-memory (`List<Subscription>`) — no persistence between sessions (FR-006)  
**Testing**: xUnit — unit tests for service classes; integration tests for API endpoints  
**Target Platform**: Local browser on Windows, macOS, or Linux (localhost, single user)  
**Project Type**: Web application — ASP.NET Core API backend + Blazor WebAssembly frontend  
**Performance Goals**: Subscription added in < 30 s from launch (SC-001); list updates within 1 s of submit (SC-002)  
**Constraints**: In-memory only; no network operations; no URL validation; no platform-specific APIs  
**Scale/Scope**: Single local user, POC/demonstration; 2 user stories, 1 entity

## Constitution Check

_GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design._

| #   | Principle                                                                                                                                                                                     | Gate                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Pre-Design | Post-Design |
| --- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ----------- |
| I   | **Security-First** — CORS explicitly configured (no wildcard); subscription URLs stored as opaque strings; input validated/sanitised at API boundary                                          | CORS allows only `http://localhost:5213` (`https://localhost:7025`). `Subscription` record holds `string Url`; rendered via Blazor `@` binding (HTML-encoded). `[Required]` on `AddSubscriptionRequest.Url`.           | ✅ PASS    | ✅ PASS     |
| II  | **MVP-First (YAGNI)** — In-memory storage only; no abstraction layers beyond what's needed; Blazor template cleanup completed before UI work                                                  | Singleton `SubscriptionService` with `List<Subscription>` + `lock`. Interface (`ISubscriptionService`) justified for testability. Template cleanup (`Counter.razor`, `Weather.razor`, `Home.razor`) is a Phase 2 gate. | ✅ PASS    | ✅ PASS     |
| III | **Separation of Concerns** — business logic in injectable C# service classes (not `.razor`); all API shapes as explicit DTO records                                                           | `SubscriptionService` injected into controller. `AddSubscriptionRequest` and `SubscriptionDto` records defined. `SubscriptionApiClient` service in frontend — no logic in `.razor`.                                    | ✅ PASS    | ✅ PASS     |
| IV  | **Code Quality** — PascalCase/camelCase/`_camelCase` naming; methods ≤~30 lines; no commented-out code; `<TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>`; no unjustified NuGet additions | Standard naming. All operations (Add/GetAll) are trivially short. No extra NuGet packages required for MVP.                                                                                                            | ✅ PASS    | ✅ PASS     |
| V   | **Testing** — xUnit unit tests for service (happy path + error path); integration tests for API endpoint (success + validation failure)                                                       | `SubscriptionServiceTests`: add valid URL (happy path) + add empty string (error path). `SubscriptionsControllerTests`: POST 201 + POST 400 on empty URL; GET 200.                                                     | ✅ PASS    | ✅ PASS     |

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/001-mvp-rss-reader/
├── plan.md              # This file (/speckit.plan command output)
├── research.md          # Phase 0 output (/speckit.plan command)
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1 output (/speckit.plan command)
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1 output (/speckit.plan command)
├── contracts/           # Phase 1 output (/speckit.plan command)
└── tasks.md             # Phase 2 output (/speckit.tasks command - NOT created by /speckit.plan)
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
backend/
├── RSSFeedReader.Api/
│   ├── Controllers/
│   │   └── SubscriptionsController.cs   # POST /api/subscriptions, GET /api/subscriptions
│   ├── DTOs/
│   │   ├── AddSubscriptionRequest.cs    # Input record (Url: string)
│   │   └── SubscriptionDto.cs           # Output record (Url: string)
│   ├── Models/
│   │   └── Subscription.cs              # Domain model
│   ├── Services/
│   │   ├── ISubscriptionService.cs
│   │   └── SubscriptionService.cs       # In-memory List<Subscription>
│   ├── Properties/
│   │   └── launchSettings.json          # http://localhost:5151
│   └── Program.cs                       # CORS: allows http://localhost:5213
└── RSSFeedReader.Api.Tests/
    ├── Unit/
    │   └── SubscriptionServiceTests.cs
    └── Integration/
        └── SubscriptionsControllerTests.cs

frontend/
├── RSSFeedReader.UI/
│   ├── Pages/
│   │   └── Subscriptions.razor          # @page "/" — main MVP page (only page)
│   ├── Services/
│   │   ├── ISubscriptionApiClient.cs
│   │   └── SubscriptionApiClient.cs     # HttpClient wrapper
│   ├── Layout/
│   │   └── NavMenu.razor                # Updated: removes template links
│   ├── wwwroot/
│   │   └── appsettings.json             # { "ApiBaseUrl": "http://localhost:5151/api/" }
│   ├── Properties/
│   │   └── launchSettings.json          # http://localhost:5213
│   └── Program.cs                       # Reads ApiBaseUrl from config
└── RSSFeedReader.UI.Tests/
    └── (future — Blazor component tests via bunit)
```

**Structure Decision**: Web application (backend + frontend). Backend is `backend/RSSFeedReader.Api` (port 5151); frontend is `frontend/RSSFeedReader.UI` (port 5213). A separate xUnit test project lives under `backend/RSSFeedReader.Api.Tests`. No shared library for MVP — the single DTO boundary is too thin to justify a third project.
